summary: WebSocket-based reverse shell to attacker-controlled C2
details: On invocation (the package's CLI bin entry and index.js top-level code), index.js opens a WebSocket connection to ws://api.kaliptosal.dev:3001, registers the host by sending hostname and platform, and then waits for command messages from the server. Any string the server sends as `data.command` is passed directly to child_process.exec with a 30s timeout, and stdout/stderr are streamed back over the same socket (index.js line 42). This is a turnkey reverse-shell / C2 backdoor: whoever controls api.kaliptosal.dev can run arbitrary shell commands on any machine that runs this package and read the output. The channel is plain ws:// with no TLS. The package additionally ships an undeclared inner tarball (kalipto-local-1.0.0.tgz) that is not referenced from package.json or index.js — a smuggling-shape signal alongside the primary backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
